title: aphyllon cooperi subsp. palmeri
intro: Aphyllon cooperi subsp. palmeri, also known as Palmer's aphyllon, is a rare and endangered succulent plant native to the southwestern United States. This plant is a type of desert marigold, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and greenish-gray stems. The medicinal properties of Palmer's aphyllon include its ability to induce vomiting, relieve nausea, and act as a purgative. The health benefits of this plant include its use in treating digestive issues, such as constipation and indigestion, and its potential antiseptic properties, which can be utilized through herbal preparations like teas, tinctures, or infusions.
